title: The Doctrine of the National Ownership of Souls
summary: “To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect.”Heb 12:23  There is a teaching floating around that declares a Christian belongs to the church that first baptized and anointed a person exclusively. This teaching further misuses the apostle Paul’s teaching and states that just as Sarah is the spiritual mother of Israel, the local church where one receives salvation becomes a “Sarah” with legitimate claims on the loyalty of the members. Finally, they misappropriate the type and shadow teaching in Genesis 2:21 to justify their “ownership” of their church members. For the sake of convenience, let us call this doctrine the “doctrine of national ownership of Apostolic souls”. None of the above stands up to scriptural scrutiny, and we need to address each one by one. The Lord Jesus will hold us responsible for what we say about souls; he alone gives birth to spiritual souls through the agency of his precious flesh and blood for souls.
